title: Crunchy Middle Eastern Egg Yolks Tagine
description: A flexitarian-friendly solo dinner dish featuring jammy egg yolks in a crunchy crust, cooked to perfection in a tandoor clay pot. This Middle Eastern inspired recipe combines the richness of egg yolks with the crunch of toasted breadcrumbs and the warmth of spices.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor clay pot to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a bowl, mix together the breadcrumbs, cumin, paprika, salt, black pepper, and sumac.
In a separate bowl, gently place the egg yolks, being careful not to break them.
Spoon the breadcrumb mixture over the egg yolks, making sure to cover them completely.
Drizzle the olive oil over the breadcrumbs, making sure they are evenly coated.
Place the egg yolks in the preheated tandoor clay pot and cook for 12 minutes.
After 12 minutes, reduce the heat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and continue cooking for an additional 8 minutes.
Remove the egg yolks from the pot and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the egg yolks hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
